sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Cercle Brugge have been inconsistent in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, showing a strong attack (10 goals scored) but recent struggles to secure wins. KAA Gent have a similar mixed record with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, scoring 11 goals but conceding in most matches. Both teams have shown defensive vulnerabilities recently.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, Cercle Brugge have not lost to KAA Gent, with a 2-1 win at home and a 1-1 draw away. Matches between these teams often see both sides scoring, with over 2.5 goals in most encounters.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Cercle Brugge rank 13th with a balanced attack and defense (10 goals scored, 9 conceded), excelling in big chances created (3rd in league). KAA Gent, positioned 8th, have similar stats but struggle with late goals conceded, as seen in their goal distribution. Both teams average around 2 yellow cards per game and generate a high number of corners in head-to-heads.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Cercle Brugge’s home advantage at Jan Breydel Stadium and their ability to score late (as per goal distribution) could be crucial. KAA Gent’s tendency to concede first (5/6 matches) and lack of clean sheets against Cercle Brugge might tilt the game in favor of the hosts.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Cercle Brugge and KAA Gent at Jan Breydel Stadium ended with an action-packed 2-2 scoreline. Both teams showcased their attacking intent, with goals coming at regular intervals. Cercle Brugge struck through a penalty and a well-timed header, while KAA Gent responded with clinical finishes to level the game. The match has been highly competitive, with neither side able to establish clear dominance. With the score tied at the break, both teams have everything to play for in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance comparison
halftimeSections.text: Cercle Brugge showed more penetration in the final third during the first half, creating a higher number of significant opportunities. Their ability to enter the penalty area frequently has been a key factor in their attacking threat. On the other hand, KAA Gent held a slight edge in ball possession and demonstrated better success in aerial and overall duels, which helped them maintain balance despite fewer entries into dangerous areas. Discipline has been a concern for Gent with more bookings, potentially impacting their approach in the second half. Both teams have shown defensive vulnerabilities, conceding twice each, which suggests an open game ahead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players analysis
halftimeSections.text: For Cercle Brugge, Edgaras Utkus has been a standout performer, finding the net with a header and contributing defensively. Steve Ngoura also made his mark by confidently converting a penalty, showing composure under pressure. On the Gent side, Michał Skóraś has been exceptional, scoring and assisting to keep his team in contention. Atsuki Ito’s timely goal just before the break was crucial in maintaining momentum for Gent. These players will likely be pivotal in deciding the outcome in the second half, as both teams look to exploit defensive lapses.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw several swings in attacking momentum between the two sides. Cercle Brugge started strongly, dominating the early phases with consistent pressure and frequent attacking spells in the opening minutes. Gent responded with brief bursts of intent, gradually building pressure mid-half to create dangerous moments. As the half progressed, Cercle Brugge regained control with sustained attacking waves, particularly around their goals. Towards the end of the half, both teams traded blows, with Gent pushing hard before Cercle Brugge mounted a strong response leading up to the whistle. This back-and-forth nature indicates a second half where momentum could shift quickly.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given the open nature of the first half, the second half is likely to see both teams pushing for a winner. Cercle Brugge will aim to capitalize on their ability to create big chances, potentially targeting Gent’s defense with quick transitions into the final third. KAA Gent, with a slight edge in possession and physical battles, might focus on controlling the tempo and exploiting set-piece opportunities. Defensive adjustments will be crucial for both sides to avoid further concessions. With the scoreline level, tactical substitutions could play a significant role in breaking the deadlock.
